subject: Nikola Tesla - Comprehensive Verified Knowledge Base

early_life.education: University education with extraordinary dedication (1875+). Attended physics lectures and made early observations about alternating current while watching a Gramme dynamo demonstration.
early_life.family_influence: Mother was an inventor of the first order; father was a priest. Both parents' influence credited for his success.

alternating_current_ac_system.description: Designed and developed the modern AC (alternating current) electricity supply system
alternating_current_ac_system.significance: Foundational to modern electrical power distribution

ac_motor.description: Invented the first practical alternating current motor based on rotating magnetic fields

tesla_coil.description: Electrical transformer that steps up voltage to very high levels and steps down current. Still used in modern radio technology.

rotating_magnetic_field_discovery.description: Discovery that rotating magnetic fields could be generated and used to drive motors
rotating_magnetic_field_discovery.impact: Led to AC motor invention and AC power generation

wireless_transmission.description: Ambitious theories and work on interplanetary communication and wireless power transmission
wireless_transmission.status: Theoretical and experimental; some successful demonstrations

teleautomatic_boat.description: Invented a remote-controlled boat, an early form of wireless control technology
teleautomatic_boat.demonstration: Public demonstration at Madison Square Garden to prove concept

tesla_tower_wardenclyffe.description: Experimental wireless transmission tower and laboratory complex
tesla_tower_wardenclyffe.status: Partially completed; now a historical site (Tesla Science Center)

thomas_edison.details: Arrived in America in 1884 to work with Edison briefly before parting ways
thomas_edison.context: Famous rivalry in the 'War of Currents' (AC vs DC)

george_westinghouse.details: Westinghouse fully recognized Tesla's brilliance and initiated collaboration
george_westinghouse.significance: Westinghouse helped commercialize Tesla's AC system

career_success.fame_level: More famous than Edison during the 1890s
career_success.recognition: Polyphase AC electric power invention earned him worldwide fame
career_success.financial_outcome: Earned worldwide fame but did not achieve corresponding fortune
career_success.circle: At his zenith, his friends included poets, scientists, industrialists, and financiers

new_york_residency.memorial: Nikola Tesla Corner designated at 40th Street and 6th Avenue in Manhattan

scientific_contributions_summary.primary_achievement: Design and development of the modern AC electricity supply system
scientific_contributions_summary.lasting_legacy: AC power systems remain the global standard. Tesla coil still in use. Theories on wireless transmission still studied.

unverified_claims: Some modern claims about Tesla's abilities are exaggerated or fictional
Popular culture has created myths around certain projects
Recommend: Cross-reference extraordinary claims with primary sources

research_notes.methodology: Verified against 8 authoritative sources: Britannica, Wikipedia, Franklin Institute, History.com, Biography.com, Library of Congress, U.S. Department of Energy, Tesla Science Center
research_notes.confidence_level: High confidence on biographical and major technical achievements
unconfirmed_areas: Some experimental wireless transmission claims require primary documentation
Tesla Tower capabilities were theoretical; actual performance unverified
